name: Ascorbic Acid
description: A naturally occurring antioxidant that helps protect cells and is commonly used as a preservative to prevent oxidation.
dietary_notes: Suitable for vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free diets.
possible_allergens: Generally considered safe; rare sensitivity may occur in high doses.
origin: Typically derived from citrus fruits or produced synthetically via fermentation.

name: Carrageenan
description: A fiber extracted from red seaweed that acts as a thickener and stabilizer in many dairy and non-dairy products.
dietary_notes: Suitable for vegan and vegetarian diets; compliant with halal and kosher standards.
possible_allergens: May cause digestive discomfort in sensitive individuals when consumed in large amounts.
origin: Harvested from species of red seaweed such as Chondrus crispus.

name: Citric Acid
description: A weak organic acid found naturally in citrus fruits, commonly used as a flavor enhancer and preservative.
dietary_notes: Suitable for vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free diets.
possible_allergens: Rare cases of intolerance have been reported, typically related to contamination during production.
origin: Produced through fermentation using Aspergillus niger or extracted from citrus fruit.

name: Soy Lecithin
description: An emulsifier derived from soybeans that helps blend ingredients that would otherwise separate, such as oil and water.
common_uses: Improving shelf life of baked goods
dietary_notes: Suitable for vegetarian and gluten-free diets; often avoided by individuals limiting soy intake.
possible_allergens: Contains soy and should be avoided by people with soy allergies unless labeled as highly refined.
origin: Typically extracted from soybean oil during processing.

name: Xanthan Gum
description: A polysaccharide produced by fermenting sugars with Xanthomonas bacteria, used to thicken and stabilize foods.
common_uses: Providing structure to gluten-free baked goods
dietary_notes: Suitable for vegan and vegetarian diets; naturally gluten-free.
possible_allergens: May cause digestive discomfort in large amounts; typically safe for most individuals.
origin: Produced via controlled fermentation and purified before use in food products.
